title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on forgiveness as a historical and social mechanism. The discussion appears to examine forgiveness through philosophical and historical lenses, questioning its role and implications in society.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer on forgiveness treated as a historical and social mechanism rather than a purely personal virtue. The conversation examines forgiveness through philosophical and historical lenses, questioning its role and implications in society. The framing suggests that forgiveness has not always functioned as an unambiguous good, and that its social uses merit scrutiny.
